Package io.forja.components.validation.fxValidator

Class FxValidator<T>

java.lang.Object
io.forja.components.validation.fxValidator.FxValidator<T>
Type Parameters:
T - value type
public class FxValidator<T> extends Object
A sequential rule engine for validating a single value against a chain of Rules. Runs rules in order, returns on the first non-null error message. No scene graph dependencies — pure logic, safe to unit test.

Convenient built-in rule factories are exposed as static methods: required(), minLength(int, String), matches(String, String), email(String), etc.

Builder usage:

     
      FxValidator<String> emailValidator = FxValidator.<String>builder()
          .rule(FxValidator.required())
          .rule(FxValidator.email("Must be a valid email"))
          .build();
      String err = emailValidator.validate("bad"); // "Must be a valid email"
See Also:
  • Rule
  • FxValidator.Builder

    Rule: rejects null/blank strings ("Required").

    Rule: rejects null/blank strings with the given message.

    Validates the given value against every rule in order.

  • Method Details

    • validate

      public String validate(T value)
      Validates the given value against every rule in order.
      Parameters:
      value - candidate
      Returns:
      first error message, or null if all rules pass

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid(T value)
      Returns whether the value is valid (no rule fires).

    • getRules

      public List<Rule<T>> getRules()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the configured rules.

    • builder

      public static <T> FxValidator.Builder<T> builder()
      Returns a new FxValidator.Builder for constructing an FxValidator.
      Type Parameters:
      T - value type
      Returns:
      a new FxValidator.Builder instance

    • of

      @SafeVarargs public static <T> FxValidator<T> of(Rule<T>... rules)
      Convenience — creates a validator from a variadic rule list.

    • required

      public static Rule<String> required()
      Rule: rejects null/blank strings ("Required").

    • required

      public static Rule<String> required(String message)
      Rule: rejects null/blank strings with the given message.

    • minLength

      public static Rule<String> minLength(int min, String message)
      Rule: minimum string length (only enforced when non-null).
